mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering recession fears that force institutional deleveraging and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in headline inflation and contraction in consumer credit demand indicates stagflationary pressure, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns with greater than 55% accuracy over a 3-year backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust order books, signaling earnings growth that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below historical norms signals collapsing consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline does not correlate with negative 20-day forward returns, with a correlation coefficient below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth coupled with expanding real GDP indicates robust aggregate demand, driving upward earnings revisions and boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day trading horizon.
failure_condition: Combined signal fails to outperform baseline SPX returns in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, yielding a Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL exceeding 6.0% annualized growth forces aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, raising real interest rates and compressing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: High inflation readings do not lead to statistically significant negative forward returns, with a p-value exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in consumer credit balances during credit cycle upswings fuels household spending and corporate investment, supporting equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Credit growth decouples from equity returns during liquidity-driven rallies, yielding a negative predictive rank correlation.
